problem_summary: Given the displacement function S(t) = 2t^3 - 5t^2 (in meters) for a particle moving along a straight line, determine the instantaneous velocity at time t = 3 seconds.
candidate_methods: Compute the derivative of S(t) with respect to t to obtain velocity function v(t)
risks: OCR error in exponent (e.g., misread t^3 as t^2 or vice versa)
Sign error in coefficients (e.g., -5t^2 misread as +5t^2)
Unit mismatch (time in seconds, displacement in meters)
Misinterpretation of 'instantaneous velocity' as average velocity
